key_findings: Router (39.85%) beats both XGBoost (63.24%) and GNN alone (46.01%) — per-sample routing is strictly better than either single model

Per-tier validation: on Tier 1 (671 unseen-arch samples) GNN 50.5% beats XGBoost 82.4% by 32 pts → Router correctly picks GNN. On Default (245 in-dist samples) XGBoost 10.7% beats GNN 33.7% by 23 pts → Router correctly picks XGBoost. Router's choice is optimal on every tier.

Tier 2 (SHAP-driven feature OOD) fires 0% in this dataset because graph aggregate features are graph-level constants; included as defense-in-depth for production deployments where fine-tuned variants might drift
Router contribution: by picking XGBoost on the 26.7% in-distribution subset where GNN underperforms by 23 pts, Router shaves 6.3 MAPE points off GNN-alone performance (46.01 - 39.85 ≈ 23 × 0.267)
